medium.name: Medium
description: ## Medium
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/30›

Mediums channel spirits into themselves, using their own bodies as vessels for astral entities spawned from myths and legends. A medium balances his need for the spirits' power with the danger of allowing such beings influence over his mind.
**Role:** Mediums are very versatile, filling whatever role the party needs at the moment by channeling the right spirit.
::class{any hd=8 wealth=4 skp=4 bluff craft diplo fly heal intm ka kp kr ling per perf prof sm spc umd}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th |
| 1st | +0 | +0 | +0 | +2 | Knacks, spirit, spirit bonus +1, spirit power (lesser), spirit surge 1d6 | - | - | - | - |
| 2nd | +1 | +0 | +0 | +3 | Shared seance, taboo | - | - | - | - |
| 3rd | +2 | +1 | +1 | +3 | Haunt channeler | - | - | - | - |
| 5th | +3 | +1 | +1 | +4 | Location channel | 1 | - | - | - |
| 6th | +4 | +2 | +2 | +5 | Spirit power (intermediate) | 1 | - | - | - |
| 7th | +5 | +2 | +2 | +5 | Connection channel | 1 | 1 | - | - |
| 11th | +8/+3 | +3 | +3 | +7 | Spirit power (greater) | 2 | 1 | 1 | - |
| 20th | +15/+10/+5 | +6 | +6 | +12 | Astral beacon, spirit bonus +6, spirit surge 1d10 | 4 | 4 | 3 | 2 |
### Spells Known
| Level | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th |
::prof{simple light medium}
::spellAb{caster=medium type=psychic stat=Charisma limitedFull medium trade53 learn}
::cantrips{c=knacks}

:::ab{title="Spirit (Su)" jl=Spirit icon=power-boost flavor="A medium serves as a vessel to channel spirits - astral echoes of powerful souls from ages past that live on in myth and legend." imp6="You also gain the spirit's intermediate spirit power." imp11="You also gain its greater spirit power." imp17="You also gain its supreme spirit power." special="After 24 hours, you lose contact with the channeled spirit and can perform another seance." action=Ability}

Each day, you can channel a ‹ability/spirit<s› through a ritual known as a seance. Seances take 1 hour to perform and require your concentration. At the end of a seance, you invite a local spirit to inhabit you and serve as your spirit for that day. You gain the spirit's listed seance boon and lesser spirit power for 24 hours.

Though you can choose the legend to which the spirit you channel belongs, you must channel that spirit from an appropriate location. Each spirit has a favored location it usually inhabits, though spirits may also be present at other locations appropriate to their legends; such locations are listed in each legend's **Favored Locations** entry.

You can invite your allies to participate in your seance: *shared seance* and certain spirit powers affect other participants. A character counts as participating so long as she maintains physical contact with another participating character and willfully opens herself to the spirit; unlike you, other participating characters can take other actions during this time. Only creatures with an Intelligence score of at least 3 can participate in a seance.

In addition to granting power to you, a channeled spirit can influence you. By channeling a spirit, you allow the spirit to gain 1 point of @HL[influence] over you. If you lose that 1 point of influence, you lose contact with the spirit, though you are still unable to perform a new seance until the normal 24 hour period has elapsed since your last seance. When the spirit leaves after the 24-hour duration and before the next seance, the spirit's influence over you resets to 0. Certain abilities allow you to gain additional power in exchange for allowing the spirit more influence over you.

When a spirit gains at least @HL[3 points of influence] over you, the dual impulses housed within the same body cause you to meld with your spirit's consciousness. The resulting muddled sense of self and struggle for control causes you to take a -2 penalty on initiative checks as well as a specific additional penalty listed in each spirit's entry. However, the spirit's mental presence grants you a +4 bonus against possession effects and a +2 bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects that are not related to possession.

If a spirit ever attains @HL[5 or more points of influence] over you, you completely lose control to the spirit. You effectively become an NPC under the GM's control until after you awaken the next day with the spirit gone.

During your seance, you can choose to @HL[channel a weaker spirit] to gain its help without incurring as much of its influence. If you do so, you can forgo any number of spirit powers, starting with your most powerful spirit power. For each spirit power you forgo, you can use your *spirit surge* ability twice without incurring influence.

::ab[Spirit Bonus (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost passive="When you channel a spirit, you gain a bonus on certain checks and to certain statistics, depending on the spirit. This bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› of your medium level."}

::ab[Spirit Surge (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=def passive="After failing a d20 roll that was modified by your spirit bonus, you can allow your spirit to gain 1 additional point of influence over you in order to add 1d6 to the check's result without taking an action. This can cause the check to succeed instead of fail. You must be conscious and aware to use this ability, and you can use this ability at most once per round." imp10="Your spirit surge die increases to 1d8." imp20="You spirit surge die increases to 1d10."}
::ab[Shared Seance (Su)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=boost passive="All allies who participate in your seance gain the channeled spirit's seance boon for 24 hours."}

:::ab{jl=Taboo title="Taboo (Ex)" l=2 icon=boost action=Ability special="If a spirit attains 5 points of influence because of a violated taboo, instead of possessing you, the spirit leaves your body entirely, and you lose access to your *spirit* powers, *spirit bonus,* and *spirit surge* abilities until the next time you can perform a seance. Banishing a spirit in this way damages your connection to that spirit's legend; you cannot channel spirits of that legend for 1 week, and the next time you channel a spirit of that legend, it starts with 2 points of influence over you instead of 1."}

During your daily seance, you can accept a taboo in order to appease your spirit. Each spirit lists example taboos, and the GM may allow additional taboos as well. Accepting a taboo allows you to use your spirit surge ability twice without incurring influence. You can select only a single taboo.

You are not magically prevented from breaking your taboo, but if you break the taboo for any reason, you take a -2 penalty on attack rolls, damage rolls, ability checks, skill checks, and saving throws for 1 hour and your spirit gains 1 point of influence over you. If you continue violating the taboo while taking the penalty, the duration of the penalty extends, but the spirit doesn't gain additional influence over you.

::ab[Haunt Channeler (Su)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=boost-melee passive="You can always act on the surprise round against a haunt." order=pa ability="Your touch funnels a portion of the haunt into yourself, dealing 1d6 points of damage to the haunt per 2 medium levels you possess as if via positive energy and allowing you to ask the haunt a single question. The haunt can answer the question however it chooses. Additionally, you can forgo your saving throw against a haunt's effects to funnel the haunt entirely into yourself. If you do so, you suffer the haunt's full effect, but the haunt doesn't affect anyone else (even if it normally would). The haunt then gains control of your body for 24 hours. Once per minute, you can attempt to end this possession early by succeeding at a Will save (DC = 20 + 1/2 your medium level)."}

::ab[Location Channel (Sp)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=power ability="You can perform a special seance at the site of a person's death - or a location that was precious to that person in life - to call forth that person's spirit into your body so your allies can ask it questions. The effects are similar to the spell ‹spell/call spirit›, except that the duration is 1 round per medium level and the spirit possesses your body instead of appearing in a wispy form. The other participants of the seance must ask the questions." special="Once you attempt to call a spirit, you cannot attempt to call the same spirit again for 24 hours. Once per round, you can attempt to end the seance early by succeeding at a Will save (DC = 20 + 1/2 your medium level)."}

::ab[Connection Channel (Sp)]{jl=strip l=7 icon=boost ability="You can use your *location channel* ability at any location, so long as you possess some connection to the deceased. If another participant in the seance is more familiar with the deceased than you, you can substitute that participant's familiarity in place of your own to determine the DC of the *call spirit* effect."}

::ab[Propitiation (Su)]{jl=strip l=9 icon=def ability="You have learned hidden techniques and rites that allow you to mollify your channeled spirit once per day, reducing its influence over you by 1 point (to a minimum of 1 point of influence). The details of the propitiation ritual vary based on the individual spirit, but the process always takes around 10 minutes to perform and it never requires any additional cost (such as an offering of wealth or a live sacrifice)."}

::ab[Ask the Spirits (Sp)]{jl=strip l=13 icon=magic ability="You can send your consciousness to the Astral Plane any number of times per day to ask the spirits for advice, as if using ‹spell/contact other plane› to contact the Astral Plane. You automatically succeed at the Intelligence check to avoid the possible decrease to your Intelligence and Charisma."}

::ab[Astral Journey (Sp)]{jl=strip l=14 icon=magic ability="You can enter a deep coma in order to project yourself onto the Astral Plane as ‹spell/astral projection›, except you can project only yourself, you can't enter other planes, and your projected gear has no magical properties."}

::ab[Trance of Three (Su)]{jl=strip l=15 icon=boost order=wf swift="You can allow your spirit to gain 1 point of influence over you in order to allow a portion of a legendary spirit from another legend to enter you for 1 round per level, granting you the intermediate spirit power from another legend." free="You can end the *trance of three* early." special="If the granted power has a limited number of uses per day, you must keep track of the remaining uses even after the secondary spirit is gone; if you call the secondary spirit again that day, it can grant the remaining uses of that power. If you use an ability that incurs influence, your primary spirit gains that amount of influence over you, not the secondary spirit. You don't gain the benefits of your spirit bonus from the secondary spirit."}

::ab[Spacious Soul (Su)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=18 order=ins flavor="A medium's soul is spacious enough to provide a foothold for his fallen allies, if he so chooses." icon=power-magic immediate="If one of the allies who participated in your seance that day dies within line of effect and line of sight of you, you can accept that ally's spirit within you. Since the ally inhabits the portion of your soul normally reserved for a spirit, this suppresses your *spirit, spirit bonus,* and *spirit surge* abilities as long as you maintain this ability." special="If the ally returns to life, this ability immediately ends. Otherwise, you can maintain this ability indefinitely." note="The ally's spirit and you share the your body and can decide each round on the your turn which of you will take the you actions for that round. If you don't agree on who should act, you attempt opposed Charisma checks to determine who is dominant for the next hour, and the dominant character decides who acts on each round. The ally's spirit has all the spells and limited use abilities (such as uses of *channel energy, ki,* and *panache*) that it possessed at the time of death, but it is incapable of regaining any abilities, points, spells, or uses of abilities it expends. When the ally's spirit acts, it uses the ally's base attack bonus, caster level, and special abilities (such as weapon proficiency), but uses your physical ability scores." standard="You can end this effect early, though if the ally is unwilling to depart, you must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the ally's hit dice + the ally's Charisma modifier) or the attempt fails and the ally becomes dominant for 24 hours."}

::ab[Spirit Mastery (Ex)]{l=19 flavor="The medium become a legend among spirits, particularly lesser spirits." icon=boost ability="You can use your spirit surge ability an additional two times per day without incurring influence, regardless of whether you accept a taboo or channels a weaker spirit. When channeling a weaker spirit, you can use your spirit surge ability an additional four times per day for each power you forgo, instead of an additional two times."}

::ab[Astral Beacon (Su)]{l=20 flavor="A medium is an open connection to the Astral Plane and a shining beacon for spirits." icon=power-boost free="You can channel spirits of any of the five legends you did not contact via your seance. This ability lasts for 1 round and grants access to the intermediate, greater, and supreme spirit powers of the chosen spirits." special="Unlike trance of three, astral beacon allows spirits of the chosen legends (for instance, archmage) to gain influence over you until 24 hours from when you contacted your primary spirit. Also unlike trance of three, if you would incur influence by using one of the new spirits' abilities, that influence is added to your total influence from that spirit rather than from your primary spirit."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Hardened Soul (Su)]{deepMagics capstone="At 20th level, the medium's soul has calluses from all the times some spirit or another has tried to take over. The medium does not become an NPC until his influence with a spirit reaches 8, and his spirit surge dice increases to 2d8."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Catfolk:** Add 1/3 to the result of any spirit surge die that the medium rolls.[^A]
**Dwarf:** Gain a +1/2 bonus on saving throws against possession and a +1/2 bonus on saving throws to end haunt channeler, location channel, and spacious soul.[^B]
**Elf:** Gain a +1/3 bonus on all skill checks using occult skill unlocks.[^B]
**Gillman:** Increase the medium's spirit bonus by 1/6.[^C]
**Gnome:** Gain a +1/2 bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive skill checks against spirits, undead, phantoms, and creatures native to the Astral Plane.[^D]
**Grippli:** Add 1/4 to any checks modified by the medium's spirit surge ability.[^E]
**Half-elf:** Gain a +1/3 bonus on all skill checks using occult skill unlocks.[^D]
**Halfling:** Increase the bonus the medium gains from its seance boon by 1/3 point. This doesn't increase the bonus allies gain from shared seance.[^D]
**Half-orc:** When gaining a taboo, the medium can use spirit surge without incurring influence an additional 1/4 time per day.[^D]
**Human:** When gaining a taboo, the medium can use spirit surge without incurring influence an additional 1/4 time per day.[^D]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^F]
**Shabti:** Add 1/5 to the bonus allies gain from shared seance. This doesn't increase the medium's seance boon.[^G]
**Tengu:** Add 1/4 to the bonus on checks when the medium uses the spirit surge ability.[^H]
**Wayang:** When gaining a taboo, the medium can use spirit surge without incurring influence an additional 1/4 time per day.[^I]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Fiend Keeper]{c=medium r="Alignment; Spirit; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Ask the Spirits" e="(Grippli only) Fiend keepers serve as a vessel to contain one of the world's evil spirits so that it cannot inflict further harm."}

::archetype[Kami Medium]{c=medium r="Spirit; Taboo; Spellcasting; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Ask the Spirits; Astral Journey" e="Some mediums channel the power of nature spirits called kami rather than the spirits of legend."}

::archetype[Medium of the Master]{c=medium r="Class Skills; Armor Proficiency; Spirit; Spirit Surge; Spirit Power (Lesser); Shared Seance; Spirit Power (Intermediate); Trance of Three; Spirit Power (Supreme)" e="A medium of the master forgoes a connection with most spirits to form a bond with the spirits of great martial artists."}

::archetype[Nexian Channeler]{c=medium r="Spirit; Knacks; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Propitiation; Ask the Spirits; Astral Journey; Trance of Three; Spacious Soul; Spirit Mastery; Astral Beacon" e="The archmage Nex long ago created an astral reflection of himself from which he can draw additional power. This echo now serves as an appropriate spirit for well-trained mediums to channel."}

::archetype[Outer Channeler]{c=medium r="Spirit; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Connection Channel; Location Channel; Ask the Spirits; Astral Journey" e="Outer channelers invite extraplanar entities to inhabit them, allowing the likes of angels and demons to use them as vessels in exchange for power."}

::archetype[Reanimated Medium]{c=medium r="Spirit; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Spacious Soul; Astral Beacon" e="Sometimes a departed soul destined for legend gains a second chance at life by possessing his own revived body."}
::archetype[Relic Channeler]{c=medium r="Spirit; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel" e="A relic channeler carries relics of legend around with her."}

::archetype[Rivethun Spirit Channeler]{c=medium r="Spellcasting; Spirit; Spirit Bonus; Spirit Surge; Taboo; Connection Channel" e="Rivethun spirit channelers are mediums who practice the ancient dwarven philosophy of the rivethun, which teaches that power lies in the connection between physical bodies and immaterial souls."}

::archetype[Spirit Dancer]{c=medium r="Spirit; Spirit Bonus; Spirit Surge; Taboo; Shared Seance; Trance of Three; Spacious Soul; Astral Beacon" e="A spirit dancer enacts an exuberant dance to the spirits, accepting many roles in the shifting dance and changing dance partners quickly."}

::archetype[Spirit Eater]{c=medium r="Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel" e="Guardians of their communities, spirit eaters absorb the dead to channel their power and serve as a line of defense against all manner of incorporeal yokai."}

::archetype[Storm Dreamer]{c=medium r="Spirit Surge; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel" e="Storm dreamers serve the Storm Kindlers by communing with their mystical predecessors. They serve as spiritual advisors for the organization, and are valued for the lore and wisdom they gain during their trances."}

::archetype[Storyteller]{c=medium r="Spirit; Spirit Bonus; Spirit Surge; Shared Seance; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Propitiation; Trance of Three; Connection Channel; Spirit Mastery; Astral Beacon" e="While all mediums connect to legendary spirits, storyteller mediums take their obsession with legends to the extreme, suffusing their lives with epic tales at the cost of some of their spiritual power."}

::archetype[Uda Wendo]{c=medium r="Class Skills; Spirit; Shared Seance; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel" e="Uda wendo are powerful mediums who are sensitive to the presence and desires of the wendo, powerful and mysterious entities that walk Golarion while shaping fate and guiding destiny."}

::archetype[Vessel of the Failed]{c=medium r="Spirit; Spirit Mastery; Spirit Surge; Taboo" e="A vessel of the failed channels the spirits of those who attempted the Test of the Starstone but failed; these spirits grant the medium abilities wrought from the spirits' ultimate shortcomings."}

::archetype[Voice of the Void]{c=medium r="Spirit; Taboo; Haunt Channeler" e="Some mediums delve deeply into the spirit world and make contact with entities that exist outside the boundaries of the sane multiverse."}

samurai.name: Samurai
description: ## Samurai
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/18›

Few warriors are more dedicated to honor and the code of the warrior than the samurai. Trained from an early age in the art of war and sworn to the service of a lord, the samurai holds a position of power and respect in many lands, often serving as the voice and justice of the local noble. The samurai takes on his training with zeal, learning the way of the blade (typically a katana), the bow, and the horse. Some even learn how to effectively use firearms, if they are available. The samurai is often the most trusted soldier in his lord's employ. In him, the common folk see honor and sacrifice. He is an honorable warrior, dedicated to the realm and the leaders that guide it.

**Role:** While typically sworn to the service of a lord, a samurai is usually given free reign as to how he performs that service. As such, samurai can sometimes be found with other adventurers, taking the fight to the enemies of their masters. Other samurai become ronin, striving to serve an ideal without paying fealty to a lord. In either case, samurai make powerful allies, capable of withstanding nearly any harm while dispatching their foes with deadly precision. The samurai is an alternate class for the cavalier base class.
::class{any hd=10 parent="‹class/Cavalier›." wealth=5 skp=4 bluff climb craft diplo ha intm prof ride sm swim}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +1 | +2 | +0 | +0 | Challenge 1/day, mount, order, resolve |
| 2nd | +2 | +3 | +0 | +0 | Order ability |
| 3rd | +3 | +3 | +1 | +1 | Weapon expertise |
| 4th | +4 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Challenge 2/day, mounted archer |
| 7th | +7/+2 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Challenge 3/day |
| 8th | +8/+3 | +6 | +2 | +2 | Order ability |
| 9th | +9/+4 | +6 | +3 | +3 | Greater resolve |
| 11th | +11/+6/+1 | +7 | +3 | +3 | Honorable stand |
| 12th | +12/+7/+2 | +8 | +4 | +4 | Bonus feat, demanding challenge |
::prof{simple martial weaps=katana~naginata~wakizashi light medium heavy shields}

::ab[Challenge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 usage="Once a day." icon=melee flavor="A samurai can challenge a foe to combat." swift="You choose one target within sight to challenge. Your melee attacks deal extra damage when made against the target of your *challenge.* This extra damage is equal to your level. Challenging a foe requires much of your concentration. You take a -2 penalty to your Armor Class, except against attacks made by the target of your *challenge.*" special="The *challenge* remains in effect until the target is dead or unconscious or until the combat ends. Each samurai's *challenge* also includes another effect which is listed in the section describing your *order.*" imp4="You can *challenge* twice a day." imp7="You can *challenge* 3 times a day." imp10="You can *challenge* 4 times a day." imp13="You can *challenge* 5 times a day." imp16="You can *challenge* 6 times a day." imp19="You can *challenge* 7 times a day."}

:::ab{jl=Mount title="Mount (Ex)" l=1 flavor="A samurai gains the service of a loyal and trusty steed to carry him into battle." icon=power action=Ability special="Should your mount die, you may find another mount to serve you after 1 week of mourning. This new mount does not gain the *link, evasion, devotion,* or *improved evasion* special abilities until the next time you gain a level."}

Your mount functions as a druid's ‹sidekick/animal companion›, using your samurai level as your effective druid level. The creature must be one that you are capable of riding and that is suitable as a mount. You do not take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while riding your mount. The mount is always considered combat trained and begins play with ‹feat/Light Armor Proficiency› as a bonus feat. Your mount does not gain the *share spells* special ability.

Medium samurai mounts: ‹companion/camel› or a ‹companion/horse›. Small samurai mounts: ‹companion/pony› or a ‹companion/wolf›. At 4th level, you add ‹companion/boar› and ‹companion/dog› to this list. The GM may approve other animals as suitable mounts.

::ab[Order (Ex)]{jl=Order l=1 icon=power-boost-skill order=an ability="You must pledge yourself to a specific ‹ability/order<s›, typically the ‹order/order of the warrior›. The order grants you a number of bonuses, class skills, and special abilities. In addition, each order includes a number of @HLedicts that you must follow. If you violate any of these edicts, you lose the benefits from your order's *challenge* ability for 24 hours. The violation of an edict is subject to GM interpretation." note="Most samurai belong to the order of the warrior and are dedicated to their lord. Those without a lord, or who chose to abandon their lord, are ‹order/ronin›. You may instead elect to dedicate yourself to any other order, but such samurai are rare." special="A samurai who wishes to change his order must undertake a lengthy process to dedicate himself to a new cause. When this choice is made, you immediately lose all of the benefits from your old order. You must then follow the edicts of your new order for one entire level without gaining any benefits from that order. Once you have accomplished this, you gain all the bonuses from your new order. The only exception to this is when a samurai decides to become a ronin. You can elect to become a ronin immediately, losing all the benefits from your old order and replacing them with the new benefits from the ronin order. Once a ronin, however, the only way for you to change to another order is through the method described above. Note that the names of these orders might vary depending upon the campaign setting or GM's preference."}

::ab[Resolve (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 useInc="2~samurai~1" passive="You gain *resolve* that you can call upon to endure even the most devastating wounds and afflictions. Whenever you defeat the target of your challenge, you regain one daily use of your *resolve,* up to your maximum number of uses per day. Defeating the target of your challenge usually involves reducing the target to 0 hit points or fewer, but the GM might rule that an enemy who surrenders or flees the battle is also defeated. You can use this *resolve* in a number of ways." head icon=power}

::ab[Determined]{sub jl=strip icon=aid standard="You can spend one use of your *resolve* to remove the ‹misc/fatigued›, ‹misc/shaken›, or ‹misc/sickened› condition." imp8="You can now remove the ‹misc/exhausted›, ‹misc/frightened›, ‹misc/nauseated›, or ‹misc/staggered› conditions, instead." special="If the condition has a duration longer than 1 hour or is permanent, this ability removes the condition for 1 hour, at which time the condition returns."}

::ab[Resolute]{sub jl=strip icon=roll immediate="Whenever you are required to make a Fortitude or Will save, you can spend one use of your *resolve* to roll twice and take the better result. You must decide to use this ability before you roll the saving throw."}

::ab[Unstoppable]{sub jl=strip icon=def immediate="When you are reduced to fewer than 0 hit points but not slain, you can spend one use of your *resolve* to instantly ‹misc/stabilize› and remain conscious. You are staggered, but you do not fall unconscious and begin dying if you take a standard action. You do fall unconscious if you take additional damage from any source."}

::ab[Weapon Expertise (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=boost ability="Select either the katana, ‹eq-weapon/longbow›, naginata, or wakizashi. Whenever you threaten a critical hit with the selected weapon, you gain a +2 bonus on the confirmation roll." passive="Your samurai levels count as fighter levels and stack with any fighter levels you possess for the purposes of meeting the prerequisites for feats that specifically select your chosen weapon, such as ‹feat/Weapon Specialization›." free="You can draw the selected weapon as if you had the ‹feat/Quick Draw› feat." order=afp}

::ab[Mounted Archer (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=boost flavor="A samuari becomes skilled at firing ranged weapons while mounted." passive="You only take a -2 penalty on attack rolls with ranged weapons while your mount takes a double move. This penalty increases to -4 while your mount is running."}

::ab[Banner (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 flavor="A samurai's banner becomes a symbol of inspiration to his allies and companions." icon=def-boost passive="As long as your banner is clearly visible, all allies within 60 feet receive a +2 morale bonus on saving throws against fear and a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls made as part of a ‹rule/charge›." special="The banner must be at least Small or larger and must be carried or displayed by you or your mount to function." imp10="The bonus on saving throws becomes +3 and the charge bonus becomes +2." imp15="These bonuses become +4 and +3." imp20="These bonuses become +5 and +4."}
:::aside

Some samurais inspire and direct their allies with specialized tactics. A samurai can select [a different battle standard](ability/banners) upon gaining the banner class feature, and its benefits replace the normal benefits of the banner and greater banner class features.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{jl=strip l=6 icon=power ability="You gain a bonus ‹main/combat feat›. You must meet the prerequisites of this feat." imp12="You gain a second bonus combat feat. You must meet the prerequisites of this feat." imp18="You gain a third bonus combat feat. You must meet the prerequisites of this feat."}

::ab[Greater Resolve (Ex)]{jl=strip l=9 icon=def immediate="After a critical hit is confirmed against you, you can spend one use of your *resolve* to treat that critical hit as a normal hit." special="Effects that only trigger on a critical hit do not trigger when you use this ability."}

::ab[Honorable Stand (Ex)]{jl=strip l=11 flavor="A samurai can make an honorable stand, deciding to fight the target of his *challenge* to the bitter end, no matter the cost." icon=def-roll usage="Once a day." swift="You can make an honorable stand. While doing so, you are immune to the shaken, frightened, and ‹misc/panicked› conditions. You do not fall unconscious while your hit point total is below 0. Finally, if you are making an honorable stand and must make a saving throw, you can spend one daily use of your *resolve* to reroll the saving throw after the first roll is made. You must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse." special="If you are making an honorable stand and ever retreat from battle against your *challenged* foe, you lose the ability to make a *challenge* for 24 hours." imp16="You can make an *honorable stand* twice a day."}

::ab[Demanding Challenge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=12 icon=lower passive="Whenever you declare a *challenge,* your target must pay attention to the threat you pose. As long as the target is within your threatened area, it takes a -2 penalty to its AC from attacks made by anyone other than you."}

::ab[Greater Banner (Ex)]{jl="Other Abilities" flavor="A samurai's banner becomes a rallying call to his allies." icon=def passive="All allies within 60 feet receive a +2 morale bonus on saving throws against charm and compulsion spells and effects." standard="While your banner is displayed, you can wave the banner through the air, granting all allies within 60 feet an additional saving throw against any one spell or effect that is targeting them. This save is made at the original DC. Spells and effects that do not allow saving throws are unaffected by this ability. An ally cannot benefit from this ability more than once per day." order=ps}

::ab[True Resolve (Ex)]{l=17 icon=def ability="If you have at least two uses of your *resolve* remaining, you can spend all of the daily uses of your *resolve* that you have available to you to avoid death. Regardless of the source of the attack that would have killed you, you are left alive, at -1 hit points (or lower if you were already below -1), ‹misc/unconscious›, and ‹misc/stable›."}

::ab[Last Stand (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def usage="Once a day." order=uap ability="You can make a *last stand* whenever you make a *challenge.* While this *challenge* is in effect, all melee and ranged weapons deal the minimum amount of damage to you, unless the attack scored is a critical hit. In addition, you remain conscious and are not staggered while you are below 0 hit points." passive="While using this ability, you cannot be killed by melee or ranged weapons unless they are wielded by the target of your *challenge.* Attacks made by opponents that are not the target of your *challenge* deal no damage when you have 0 or fewer hit points. This ability has no effect on spells, environmental effects, supernatural abilities, or any other source of damage other than melee and ranged weapons. Such sources of damage affect you normally and can kill you (although they do not cause you to fall unconscious or to become staggered if they reduce your hit points below 0)." special="This effect lasts until the *challenge* ends or you take an offensive action against a target other than the target of your *challenge.*"}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Indomitable Spirit (Ex)]{greatBeast capstone="At 20th level, the sight of the samurai on the battlefield gives heart to even the most doomed force. The range of the samurai's banner increases by 90 feet, the bonuses offered by the banner increase by 2, and the samurai can wave his banner to use its ability as a move action instead of a standard action."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aasimar:** Add +1/4 to the samurai's bonus on damage against targets of his challenge.[^A]
**Aquatic elf:** Add 1 skill rank to the samurai's mount. If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus skill ranks.[^B]
**Catfolk:** Add +1/4 to the samurai's banner bonus.[^C]
**Dwarf:** Add +1/2 to the samurai's bonus to damage against targets of his challenge.[^D]
**Elf:** Add +1 hit point to the samurai's mount. If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^E] [^F]
**Gathlain:** Add a +1/2 bonus to the samurai's Knowledge (nature) and Knowledge (nobility) checks relating to fey courts and fey courtiers.[^G]

**Gnome:** Add +1 to the samurai's mounted base speed. In combat this has no effect unless the samurai has selected this reward five times (or another increment of five). If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains this bonus to its speed.[^H]
**Goblin:** Add +1 hit points to the samurai's mount companion. If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^I] [^J]

**Half-elf:** Add +1 foot to the samurai's mount's base speed. This option has no effect unless the samurai has selected it 5 times (or another increment of 5); a speed of 54 feet is effectively a speed of 50 feet, for example. If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new companion gains this base speed bonus.[^K]
**Halfling:** Add +1/2 to the samurai's effective class level for the purposes of determining the damage he deals when making an attack of opportunity against a challenged foe.[^L]
**Halfling:** The samurai's mount gains a +1/2 bonus on saving throws against fear effects. If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains this bonus.[^M]
**Half-orc:** Add +1 hit point to the samurai's mount companion. If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^N]
**Hobgoblin:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Intimidate checks and Ride checks.[^O]
**Human:** Add +1/4 to the samurai's banner bonus.[^P] [^Q]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^R]
**Kitsune:** Add 1/4 to the samurai's banner bonus.[^R]
**Kobold:** Add 5 feet (up to 15 feet maximum) to the samurai's mount's speed when it uses the charge or withdraw action.[^S]
**Locathah:** Add 1 hit point to the samurai's mount. If the samurai ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^T]

**Nagaji:** Add 1 foot to the base speed of the samurai's mount. This has no effect in combat unless the samurai has selected this reward five times (or another increment of five). If the samurai ever replaces this mount, the new mount gains this bonus to its speed.[^U]
**Orc:** Add +1 to the samurai's CMB when making bull rush or overrun combat maneuvers against a challenged target.[^V]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Brawling Blademaster]{c=samurai r="Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Mount; Weapon Expertise; Mounted Archer; Banner; Greater Banner" e="Brawling blademaster samurai use weapon strikes and unarmed attacks in equal measure"}

::archetype[Ironbound Sword]{c=samurai r="Weapon Expertise; Banner; Greater Banner" e="The ironbound sword's fighting style, focused on incapacitating opponents rather than killing them, can subdue a foe without causing permanent harm, leaving the soul of the samurai unstained by deaths of her enemies."}

::archetype[Sovereign Blade]{c=samurai r="Alignment; Mount; Mounted Archer; 6th, 12th, 18th-level bonus feats" e="In Tian Xia, many samurai are tutored in the ways of honor using lessons that originate from sovereign dragons and their dedication to safeguarding harmony."}

::archetype[Sword Saint]{c=samurai r="Samurai's Mount; Mounted Charge; Banner; Greater Banner" e="Sword saints hail from lands where samurai are prevalent, and are often ronin who wander the world seeking new challenges to perfect their intricate style of swordplay called iaijutsu."}

::archetype[Ward Speaker]{c=samurai r="Alignment; Resolve; Greater Resolve; True Resolve; Honorable Stand" e="The ward speaker draws power from simple rituals to honor the spirits found throughout the world so that he might better protect those entrusted to his care."}

::archetype[Warrior Poet]{c=samurai r="Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Mount; Weapon Expertise; Banner; Greater Banner; Challenge; Mounted Archer; Demanding Challange; Bonus Combat Feats" e="Warrior poets often study calligraphy, flower arrangement, poetry, and other courtly arts, but when called to battle, they treat combat as its own art form, fighting with beauty and grace."}
::archetype[Yojimbo]{c=samurai r="Mount; Weapon Expertise; Mounted Archer" e="Yojimbo are highly trained bodyguards favored by the nobles and warlords of distant Minkai."}

shaman.name: Shaman
description: ## Shaman
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/35›

While some heroes speak to gods or consort with otherworldly muses, shamans commune with the spirits of the world and the energies that exist in every living thing. These divine adventurers draw upon their power to shape the world and expand the influence of their spiritual patrons. Shamans have strong ties to natural spirits. They form powerful bonds with particular spirits, and as their power grows they learn to call upon other spirits in times of need.

**Role:** Shamans make for potent divine spellcasters, capable of using divine spells and the power of their spirits to aid their allies and destroy their foes. While they aren't the healers that clerics are, they can still fill that role when needed.
::class{any hd=8 parent="‹class/Oracle› and ‹class/witch›." wealth=3 skp=4 craft diplo fly ha heal kna kp kr prof ride spc sur}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th | 7th | 8th | 9th |
| 1st | +0 | +0 | +0 | +2 | Orisons, spirit, spirit animal, spirit magic | 3 | 1 | - | - | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| 20th | +15/+10/+5 | +6 | +6 | +12 | Hex, manifestation, wandering spirit (true) | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 |
::prof{simple light medium}
::spellAb{caster=shaman shaman stat=Wisdom type=divine type=Wisdom ritualDivine prepare}
::cantrips{c=orisons p}

::ab[Spirit (Su)]{jl=strip flavor="A shaman forms a mystical bond with the spirits of the world." icon=power-magic ability="You form a lasting bond with a single ‹ability/shaman_>spirit<s›, which grants a number of abilities and defines many of your other class features." imp1="You gain the *spirit ability* granted by your chosen spirit. You add the spells granted by that spirit to the list of spells that you can cast using *spirit magic.* You also add the hexes possessed by that spirit to the list of hexes that you can use with the *hex* and *wandering hex* class features." imp8="You gain the abilities listed in the *greater* version of your selected spirit." imp16="You gain the abilities listed for the *true* version of your selected spirit." special="If you take levels in another class that grants a mystery (such as the oracle), the spirit and mystery must match, even if that means one of them must change. Subject to GM discretion, you can change your former mystery or spirit to make them conform."}

:::ab{jl="Spirit Animal" title="Spirit Animal (Ex)" l=1 icon=power action=Ability imp1="Your spirit animal gains specific abilities, depending upon the type of spirit selected by you using your spirit class feature. These abilities affect the animal's appearance and grant it special abilities that can aid it in serving you and the spirit it represents and is connected to. These abilities are described in the *spirit animal* section of each individual *spirit* description." imp3="Your spirit animal can deliver touch spells or hexes for you. If you and the spirit animal are in contact at the time you cast a touch spell, you can designate your animal as the &quot&toucher.&quot& The spirit animal can then deliver the touch spell just as you would. If you cast another spell before the touch is delivered, the touch spell dissipates. If you activate a *hex,* your spirit animal can be used to make the touch; you don't have to be in contact with the animal to use this ability with hexes."}
You form a close bond with a spirit animal tied to your chosen *spirit.* This animal is your conduit to the spirit world, guiding you along the path to enlightenment.

You must commune with your spirit animal each day to prepare your spells. While the spirit animal does not store the spells like a witch's familiar does, the spirit animal serves as your conduit to divine power. If your spirit animal is slain, you cannot prepare new spells or use your spirit magic class feature until the spirit animal is replaced.

By communing with the incredible powers of your spirit, you forge a cherished bond with one specific servant of that spirit - known as a *spirit animal.* A spirit animal is a creature chosen by you to serve as a conduit, allowing you to more fully access the magic of your spirit on a daily basis. Your spirit animal also grants you special powers. This ability uses the same rules as the wizard's *arcane bond* class feature and is treated as a ‹sidekick/familiar›, except as noted below.

You use your level as your effective wizard level when determining the abilities of your spirit animal. You can select ‹ability/any »familiar« available to wizard»s› to serve as your spirit animal, although your spirit animal is augmented by the power of your chosen spirit. Once selected, the spirit animal cannot be changed. Although your spirit animal uses the statistics of a specific animal, it is treated as an outsider with the native subtype for the purposes of spells and abilities that affect it.

Levels of different classes that are entitled to familiars stack with shaman levels for the purpose of determining any spirit animal abilities that depend on your level. If you possess such levels in other classes, your spirit animal always uses your rules for spirit animals, not those used by familiars of other classes (such as ‹class/witch«es›, ‹class/wizard«s›, or ‹class/sorcerer«s› with the ‹sorcbloodline/arcane› bloodline). If you have witch levels, your spirit animal also serves as the conduit to your patron and stores your witch spells. Your spirit animal is treated as a familiar for the purposes of all spells, effects, and abilities that affect familiars.

If a spirit animal is lost or dies, it can be replaced after 24 hours through a special ritual that consumes material components worth 500 gp per shaman level. The ritual takes 8 hours to complete. The new spirit animal must be of the same sort of creature as the previous one.

::ab[Spirit Magic]{jl icon=magic ability="You can spontaneously cast a limited number of spells per day beyond those you prepared ahead of time. You have one spell slot per day of each shaman spell level you can cast, not including orisons. You can choose these spells from the list of spells granted by your spirits at the time you cast them. You can enhance these spells using any metamagic feat that you know, using up a higher-level spell slot as required by the feat and increasing the time to cast the spell."}

::ab[Hex]{jl flavor="A shaman learns a number of magical tricks, called hexes, which grant her powers or weaken foes." l=2 icon=magic ability="You learn one hex. You can select from any ‹ability/of these »shaman hexes› or from any of the hexes listed in the description of your chosen spirit. You cannot select a hex more than once unless noted otherwise." repeatAt="You learn a new hex.~4~8~10~12~16~18~20" special="Using a hex is a standard action that doesn't provoke an attack of opportunity unless otherwise noted. The saving throw DC to resist a hex is equal to 10 + ‹misc/half› your shaman level + your Wisdom modifier."}
:::aside
Shamans can take a ‹main/feats_>blood hex« feat› in place of a regular hex.

::ab[Wandering Spirit (Su)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=power-magic ability="You can form a temporary bond with a spirit other than the one selected using your *spirit* class feature. You must make this selection each day when preparing your spells. While this feature is active, you gain the *spirit ability* granted by the spirit. You also add the spells granted by that spirit to your list of spells that you can cast using *spirit magic.* You do not add the hexes from your *wandering spirit* to your list of hexes that you can choose from with the hex class feature." imp12="You gain the abilities listed in the *greater* version of your *wandering spirit.*" imp20="You gain the ability listed in the *true* version of your *wandering spirit.*"}

::ab[Wandering Hex]{jl=strip icon=magic l=6 ability="You can temporarily gain the use of one of the hexes possessed by either one of your spirits. You must make this selection each day when you prepare your spells. For the purposes of this ability, you can select any hex possessed by your *spirit* or *wandering spirit.* If you select it from your *wandering spirit,* you lose the hex immediately if you bond with another spirit, although you can then select a different hex to gain using this ability, from either your *spirit* or your new *wandering spirit.*" imp14="You can select two wandering hexes each day instead of one." special="This ability otherwise functions as the hex class feature."}

::ab[Manifestation (Su)]{jl=strip icon=power l=20 ability="You undergo a transformation as you manifest as a pinnacle of your main spirit. The nature of this manifestation depends on your *spirit,* and is described in its entry."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc
::altCapstone[Hex Mastery (Su)]{archFamiliar capstone="At 20th level, the shaman has learned ever more terrible hexes. She can select one ‹ability/grand hex<es› from the list available to witches."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Catfolk:** Add 1/6 to the shaman's class level for the purpose of determining the effects of one hex.[^A]
**Dwarf:** Add 1/4 to the natural armor bonus of the shaman's spirit animal.[^B]
**Elf:** Add 5 feet to the range of a chosen shaman hex. Multiple bonuses from this ability can apply to the same hex, to a maximum of an additional 30 feet for any single hex.[^B]
**Gathlain:** Add one spell from the druid spell list to the shaman's spell list. This spell must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level spell the shaman can cast.[^C]

**Ghoran:** Add one spell from the druid spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells she knows. This spell must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level spell the shaman can cast.[^D]
**Gnome:** The shaman gains 1/6 of a new shaman hex.[^E]

**Grippli:** Add one spell from the druid spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest spell level the shaman can cast.[^F]

**Half-elf:** Add one spell from the cleric spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest spell level the shaman can cast.[^E]
**Halfling:** Add 1/2 to the shaman's effective class level for the purpose of determining her spirit animal's natural armor adjustment, Intelligence, and special abilities.[^E]

**Half-orc:** Add one spell from the cleric spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest spell level the shaman can cast.[^G]

**Human:** Add one spell from the cleric spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest spell level the shaman can cast.[^G]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^H]

**Kitsune:** Add one enchantment spell from the sorcerer/ wizard spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest spell level the shaman can cast.[^H]

**Shabti:** Add one spell from the psychic spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest spell level the shaman can cast.[^I]
**Strix:** Add 1/2 to the shaman's effective class level for the purpose of determining her spirit animal's natural armor adjustment, Intelligence, and special abilities.[^J]

**Vanara:** Add one spell from the cleric spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest level the shaman can cast.[^K]

**Vine leshy:** Shamans with the nature or wood spirit add one spell from the druid's spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the shaman's spell list; she still must select this spell as one of her spells known in order to cast it. This spell must be at least 1 level lower than the highest spell level the shaman can cast.[^L]

**Wayang:** Add one spell from the sorcerer/wizard spell list that isn't on the shaman spell list to the list of spells the shaman knows. This spell must be at least 1 level below the highest spell level the shaman can cast and be of the illusion (shadow) subschool or have the darkness descriptor.[^M]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Animist]{c=shaman r="Spirit Magic Spells; 2nd, 8th, 10th, 12th, and 18th-level Hexes; Manifestation" e="Even among mystical practitioners, the animist has a strange perspective and even stranger magic. The animist perceives that all things have a spirit, including objects, constructs, illnesses, buildings, and the environment."}

::archetype[Benefactor]{c=shaman r="Spellcasting; Hexes" e="A benefactor seeks to improve the lives of others by primarily offering powerful supportive abilities and magic to her allies and others she deems worthy."}

::archetype[Crystal Tender]{c=shaman r="Spirit Animal; Wandering Spirit; 8th-level Shaman Hex; Manifestation" e="A crystal tender focuses on the spirits of metal and stone, using crystals as a means to focus the power of the spirits she venerates."}
::archetype[Deep Shaman]{c=shaman r="Spirit; Spirit Animal; Class Skills; Spirit Abilities" e="Deep shamans are tied to spirits from the depths."}

::archetype[Draconic Shaman]{c=shaman r="Spirit; Spirit Animal; 4th, 10th-level Hexes; Spirit Magic" e="Some shamans draw their powers from the might of dragons. These shamans each gain a powerful drake as an ally, and view caring for that drake as a sacred duty"}

::archetype[Grasping Vine]{c=shaman r="Spirit Animal; Spirit Magic; 2nd, 8th-level Hex" e="Grasping vine shamans are typically adherents of one of several scattered sects dedicated to the Green Mother. They revere plant life in all its forms, with a special fondness for poisonous or carnivorous plants."}

::archetype[Name-Keeper]{c=shaman r="Class Skills; Spirit Animal; Spirit Magic; Wandering Spirit; Wandering Hex" e="Name-keepers have delved into the storied histories etched into the Wall of Names and communed with the departed spirits of those whose names are memorialized on the monument."}

::archetype[Overseer]{c=shaman r="Spirit Magic; Deliver Touch Spells" e="While all shamans use their connection to the spirits of the world to draw upon otherworldly magic powers, the shamans of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings have a unique tradition in which they use the power of patron spirits to directly control their enemies."}

::archetype[Possessed Shaman]{c=shaman r="Spirit Magic; 2nd and 6th-level Hexes" e="For a possessed shaman, merely communing with the spirit world is insufficient. Instead, she invites the spirits to share her body, granting them the chance to experience corporeal existence."}

::archetype[Primal Warden]{c=shaman r="Spirit Animal; Spirit Magic; 4th, 8th, 12th-level Hex" e="A shaman who communes with spirits that linger in areas where primal magic reigns, learning to stabilize these erratic energies and draw upon them to her benefit."}

::archetype[Serendipity Shaman]{c=shaman r="Spirit Magic spells" e="The serendipitous, shamanistic rites associated with the traditional catfolk worship of the so-called &quot&spirits of creation&quot& focus upon attracting good fortune and banishing ill fortune in the name of these spirits."}

::archetype[Speaker for the Past]{c=shaman r="Spirit Animal; Wandering Spirit; Wandering Hex" e="A speaker for the past is a shaman who specifically serves as the voice for spirits from her people's history."}

::archetype[Spirit Warden]{c=shaman r="Class Skills; Spirit Magic Spells; 2nd and 10th-level Hexes" e="Not all spirits deserve reverence and respect. Some are twisted and despicable. It's a spirit warden's duty to end these spirits' existence."}

::archetype[True Silvered Throne]{c=shaman r="Class Skills; Spirit Animal; Wandering Spirit; Wandering Hex; 8th-level Hex" e="Members in good standing who have risen to a prestigious rank within the Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye, true silvered thrones have managed to discover rituals and occult secrets within the lore of their order."}

::archetype[Unsworn Shaman]{c=shaman r="Spirit; Hex; Spirit Animal; Wandering Spirit; Wandering Hex; Spirit Magic" e="An unsworn shaman never binds herself to one specific spirit, always making new deals as she deems necessary for the circumstances that she finds herself in."}

::archetype[Visionary]{c=shaman r="Wandering Spirit; Spirit Magic Spells; Wandering Hex; Greater Wandering Spirit; True Wandering Spirit" e="The visionary is a master at divination, drawing upon her intimate relationship with the spirit world to ferret out all manner of secrets and insights about the world around her and beyond."}
::archetype[Witch Doctor]{c=shaman r="Alignment; 4th, 8th, 10th, and 12th-level Hexes" e="The witch doctor is a healer who specializes in afflictions of the soul."}

warpriest.name: Warpriest
description: ## Warpriest
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/60›

Capable of calling upon the power of the gods in the form of blessings and spells, warpriests blend divine magic with martial skill. They are unflinching bastions of their faith, shouting gospel as they pummel foes into submission, and never shy away from a challenge to their beliefs. While clerics might be subtle and use diplomacy to accomplish their aims, warpriests aren't above using violence whenever the situation warrants it. In many faiths, warpriests form the core of the church's martial forces - reclaiming lost relics, rescuing captured clergy, and defending the church's tenets from all challenges.
**Role:** Warpriests can serve as capable healers or spellcasters, calling upon their divine powers from the center of the fight, where their armor and martial skills are put to the test.

::class{al="A warpriest's alignment must be within one step of his deity's, along either the law/chaos axis or the good/evil axis." noAlignmentTable hd=8 parent="‹class/Cleric› and ‹class/fighter›." wealth=5 skp=2 climb craft diplo ha heal intm ke kr prof ride sm spc sur swim}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | Sacred Weapon Damage | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
| 1st | +0 | +2 | +0 | +2 | Aura, blessings (minor), focus weapon, orisons, sacred weapon | 1d6 | 3 | 1 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 4th | +3 | +4 | +1 | +4 | Channel energy, sacred weapon +1 | 1d6 | 4 | 3 | 1 | - | - | - | - |

::prof{simple martial wExtra="The favored weapon of your deity" light medium heavy shields extra="If you worship a deity with unarmed strike as its favored weapon, you gain ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat."}
::spellAb{caster=warpriest type=divine alignLimit stat=Wisdom prepare ritualDivine}
::cantrips{c=orisons p}

::ab[Spontaneous Casting]{jl=strip flavor="A good warpriest (or a neutral warpriest of a good deity) can channel stored spell energy into healing spells that he did not prepare ahead of time." l=1 icon=magic ability="If you are good (or a neutral warpriest of a good deity), you can &quot&lose&quot& any prepared spell that is not an orison in order to cast any *cure* spell of the same spell level or lower. If you are evil (or a neutral warpriest of an evil deity), you can convert spells to *inflict* spells." special="If you are neither good nor evil and your deity is neither good nor evil, you choose whether you can convert spells into either *cure spells* or *inflict spells.* Once this choice is made, it cannot be changed. This choice also determines whether you channel positive or negative energy (see below)."}
:::aside
A *cure spell* is any spell with "cure" in its name, while an *inflict spell* is one with "inflict" in its name. These spells are ‹main/spell_>group«ed here<ings›.

::ab[Chaotic, Evil, Good, and Lawful Spells]{jl="Aligned Spells" icon=magic passive="You can't cast spells of an alignment opposed to your own or your deity's (if you have one). Spells associated with particular alignments are indicated by the chaos, evil, good, and law descriptors in their spell descriptions."}

::ab[Bonus Languages]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power passive="Your bonus language options include Abyssal, Celestial, and Infernal. These choices are in addition to the bonus languages available because of your race."}

::ab[Aura (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=aura l=1 passive="If you are a warpriest of a chaotic, evil, good, or lawful deity, you have a particularly powerful aura (as a ‹class/cleric›) corresponding to your deity's alignment (see ‹spell/detect evil›)."}

::ab[Blessings (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-magic flavor="A warpriest's deity influences his alignment, what magic he can perform, his values, and how others see him." ability="You can select two ‹ability/blessings› from among those granted by your deity (each deity grants the blessings tied to its domains), obtaining each blessing's @HLminor power." usage="3 times/day + ‹misc/half› your warpriest level" order=aus standard="Unless otherwise stated, using a blessing is a standard action that does not provoke an attack of opportunity. Each time you call upon any one of your blessings, it counts against your daily limit." imp10="You obtain the @HLmajor power of each of your blessings." special="You can select an alignment blessing (‹blessing/Chaos›, ‹blessing/Evil›, ‹blessing/Good›, or ‹blessing/Law›) only if your alignment matches that domain. If you aren't devoted to a particular deity, you still select two blessings to represent your spiritual inclinations and abilities, subject to GM approval. The restriction on alignment domains still applies."}
::ab[Focus Weapon]{jl="Focus and Sacred Weapon" l=1 icon=power ability="You receive ‹feat/Weapon Focus› as a bonus feat (you can choose any weapon, not just your deity's favored weapon)."}
:::aside
A warpriest may receive the ‹feat/Modified Weapon Proficiency› feat instead of Weapon Focus, but only if he selects his deity's favored weapon. -- *‹source/Adventurer's Armory 2« pg. 11›*

:::ab{title="Sacred Weapon (Su)" l=1 icon=boost flavor="Weapons wielded by a warpriest are charged with the power of his faith." order=paC ability="In addition to the favored weapon of your deity,you can designate a weapon as a sacred weapon by selecting that weapon with the Weapon Focus feat; if you have multiple Weapon Focus feats, this ability applies to all of them." passive="Whenever you hit with your sacred weapon, the weapon damage is based on your size and level and not the weapon type. (Only Medium warpriest damage is listed above; see the table below if you are Small or Large.) You can decide to use the weapon's base damage instead of the sacred weapon damage - this must be declared before the attack roll is made. (If the weapon's base damage exceeds the sacred weapon damage, its damage is unchanged.) This increase in damage does not affect any other aspect of the weapon, and doesn't apply to alchemical items, bombs, or other weapons that only deal energy damage." action="At 4th Level"}

You gain the ability to enhance one of your sacred weapons with divine power as a @HL[swift action]. This power grants the weapon an enhancement bonus. This bonus is equal to one-fourth of your warpriest level; see the table below. If you have more than one sacred weapon, you can enhance another on the following round by using another swift action. You can use this ability a number of rounds per day equal to your warpriest level, but these rounds need not be consecutive.
| Level | Damage (Small) | Damage (Medium) | Damage (Large) | Enhancement Bonus |

These bonuses stack with any existing bonuses the weapon might have, to a maximum of +5. You can enhance a weapon with any of the following weapon special abilities: ‹magic-enh/brilliant energy›, ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/disruption›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, and ‹magic-enh/shock›. In addition, if you are chaotic, you can add ‹magic-enh/anarchic› and ‹magic-enh/vicious›. If you are evil, you can add ‹magic-enh/mighty cleaving› and ‹magic-enh/unholy›. If you are good, you can add ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› and ‹magic-enh/holy›. If you are lawful, you can add ‹magic-enh/axiomatic› and ‹magic-enh/merciful›. If you are neutral (with no other alignment components), you can add ‹magic-enh/spell storing› and ‹magic-enh/thundering›. Adding any of these special abilities replaces an amount of bonus equal to the special ability's base cost. Duplicate abilities do not stack. The weapon must have at least a +1 enhancement bonus before any other special abilities can be added.

If multiple weapons are enhanced, each one consumes rounds of use individually. The enhancement bonus and special abilities are determined the first time the ability is used each day, and cannot be changed until the next day. These bonuses do not apply if another creature is wielding the weapon, but they continue to be in effect if the weapon otherwise leaves your possession (such as if the weapon is thrown). This ability can be ended as a @HL[free action] at the start of your turn (that round does not count against the total duration, unless the ability is resumed during the same round). If you use this ability on a ‹misc/double› weapon, the effects apply to only one end of the weapon.

::ab[Fervor (Su)]{jl=strip flavor="A warpriest can draw upon the power of his faith to heal wounds or harm foes. He can also use this ability to quickly cast spells that aid in his struggles." usage="Wis modifier times/day + 1/2 your warpriest level" icon=aid-touch-magic order=ausw ability="If you spontaneously cast *cure* spells, this ability harms undead (no save) and heals other creatures as positive energy. Otherwise, it heals undead and harms other creatures (no save) as negative energy." standard="By expending one use of this ability, you can touch a creature to heal it of 1d6 points of damage, or damage it for the same amount. Attempting to harm is a melee touch attack that provokes attacks of opportunity." swift="You can expend one use of this ability to either heal yourself (as if you touched a creature to heal it) or cast any one warpriest spell you have prepared with a casting time of 1 round or shorter. The spell can target only you, even if it could normally affect other or multiple targets. Spells cast in this way ignore somatic components and do not provoke attacks of opportunity. You do not need to have a free hand to cast a spell in this way." imp5="The damage you deal or heal becomes 2d6." imp8="The damage you deal or heal becomes 3d6." imp11="The damage you deal or heal becomes 4d6." imp14="The damage you deal or heal becomes 5d6." imp17="The damage you deal or heal becomes 6d6." imp20="The damage you deal or heal becomes 7d6."}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{jl=strip icon=power l=3 ability="You gain a bonus ‹main/combat feat› in addition to those gained from normal advancement. You must meet the prerequisites for this feat, but you treat your warpriest level as your base attack bonus (in addition to base attack bonuses gained from other classes and Hit Dice) for the purpose of qualifying for this feat. Finally, for the purposes of this feat, you can select one that have a minimum number of fighter levels as a prerequisite, treating your warpriest level as your fighter level." repeat="You gain another bonus combat feat, following the same restrictions as above.~6~3"}

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=aura-aid order=as ability="You gain the ability to ‹ability/channel energy› like a cleric, by presenting your holy (or unholy) symbol." provokes=No standard="By expending two uses of your *fervor,* you cause a ‹misc/burst› that heals (or deals damage to) all creatures in a 30-foot radius centered on you (following the same rules as your *fervor*). The amount of damage healed or dealt is equal to that of your *fervor* ability. You can choose whether or not to include yourself in this effect." special="Creatures that take damage from channeled energy must succeed at a Will saving throw to halve the damage. The save DC is 10 + 1/2 your warpriest level + your Wisdom modifier."}

:::ab{jl="Sacred Armor" title="Sacred Armor (Su)" l=7 icon=def useL=warpriest useUnit=minute useNC=1 swift="You grant your armor a +1 enhancement bonus." increment="The bonus~7~3" order=wufC free="When you grant this bonus, you can also use your *sacred weapon* ability by expending one use of your *fervor.*" action=Special}

These bonuses stack with any existing bonuses the armor might have, to a maximum of +5. You can enhance armor any of the following armor special abilities: ‹magic-enh/energy resistance› (normal, improved, and greater), ‹magic-enh/fortification› (heavy, light, or moderate), ‹magic-enh/glamered›, and ‹magic-enh/spell resistance› (13, 15, 17, and 19). Adding any of these special abilities replaces an amount of bonus equal to the special ability's base cost. For this purpose, *glamered* counts as a +1 bonus, *energy resistance* counts as +2, ‹magic-enh/improved energy resistance› counts as +4, and ‹magic-enh/greater energy resistance› counts as +5. Duplicate abilities do not stack. The armor must have at least a +1 enhancement bonus before any other special abilities can be added.

The enhancement bonus and armor special abilities are determined the first time the ability is used each day and cannot be changed until the next day. These bonuses apply only while you are wearing the armor, and end immediately if the armor is removed or leaves your possession. This ability can be ended as a @HL[free action] at the start of your turn. This ability cannot be applied to a shield.

::ab[Aspect of War (Su)]{jl=strip l=20 icon=boost-def-magic flavor="The warpriest can channel an aspect of war, growing in power and martial ability." usage="Once a day." swift="You can treat your level as your base attack bonus, gain DR 10/-, and can move at your full speed regardless of the armor you are wearing or your encumbrance. In addition, the *blessings* you call upon don't count against your daily limit during this time. This ability lasts for 1 minute."}
::h3[Ex-Warpriests]{jl}

A warpriest who grossly violates the code of conduct required by his god loses all spells and class features, except for his armor, shield, weapon proficiencies, and bonus feats. He cannot thereafter gain levels as a warpriest of that god until he atones for his deeds (see ‹spell/atonement›).
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Hammer of God (Su)]{soulChannel oldDogNewTricks capstone="At 20th level, the warpriest has become one of his deity's favorite weapons - the first tool that comes to hand when destruction is called for. The warpriest gains two additional blessings from the list offered by his deity. He can also call upon his blessings two more times each day."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}

**Aphorite:** Add 1/2 to the number of times per day the warpriest can use blessings, but he can use these additional blessings on only those from the Artifice, Community, Knowledge, Law, Magic, or Rune domains.[^A]
**Drow:** Add 1/2 point to the amount of damage dealt or healed by the warpriest's fervor ability.[^B]
**Dwarf:** Add 1/3 to the number of times per day the warpriest can use blessings, but he can only use these additional uses on blessings that affect weapons or armor.[^C]
**Elf:** Add 1/2 to the number of times per day the warpriest can use blessings, but he can use these additional blessings on only those from the Air, Animal, Charm, Magic, Plant, or Sun domains.[^D]
**Gnome:** Add 1/2 to the result of the warpriest's channeled energy when healing creatures of the animal, fey, and plant types.[^D]
**Half-elf:** Gain a +1/3 bonus on the damage dealt or healed with the warpriest's channel energy ability.[^D]
**Halfling:** Add 1/4 to the warpriest's effective level when determining the damage of his sacred weapon.[^D]
**Half-orc:** Gain a +2 bonus on rolls to stabilize when dying.[^E]
**Human:** Gain 1/6 of a new bonus combat feat.[^E]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^F]
**Nagaji:** Add 1/2 to the number of times per day the warpriest can use blessings, although he can use these additional blessings only for Charm, Magic, Nobility, Rune, or Scalykind blessings.[^G]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Calamity Caller]{c=warpriest r="Blessings; Focus Weapon; Sacred Weapon; Bonus Feats; Aspect of War" e="(Elf Only) While all elves feel a deep connection to nature, some elven priests take this bond further, using their link to call down the wrath of nature upon their enemies"}

::archetype[Champion of the Faith]{c=warpriest r="Sacred Weapon; 3rd-level Bonus Feat; Channel Energy" e="Champions of the faith are crusaders who use the power of their divine patron to annihilate the faith's enemies."}

::archetype[Cult Leader]{c=warpriest r="Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Focus Weapon; 3rd, 9th, 12th, and 15th-level Bonus Feats; Channel Energy" e="Referred to as fanatics, lunatics, or obsessives, cultists see themselves as genuine devotees of their deity."}

::archetype[Disenchanter]{c=warpriest r="Bonus Feats; Channel Energy; 6th-level Bonus Feat" e="While many warpriests focus on threats to the body, the disenchanter focuses on dangers to the mind and the soul."}

::archetype[Divine Champion]{c=warpriest r="3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th, 18th-level Bonus Feat" e="Divine champions are unswervingly devoted to their causes, and specialize in bringing battle to the enemies of their faiths."}

::archetype[Divine Commander]{c=warpriest r="Blessings; 3rd, 6th, 12th, and 15th-level Bonus Feats" e="Some warpriests are called to lead great armies and face legions of foes. These divine commanders live for war and fight for glory."}

::archetype[Feral Champion]{c=warpriest r="Blessings; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor" e="When a warpriest devotes himself to a god of the natural world, he is sometimes blessed with supernatural powers that allow him to evoke animalistic power and fury."}

::archetype[Fist of the Godclaw]{c=warpriest r="Alignment; Deity; Blessings; 3rd, 6th, 12th-level Bonus Feat; Aspect of War" e="Devoted to the divine control of law above all else, these zealots seek to establish absolute order."}

::archetype[Forgepriest]{c=warpriest r="Blessings; Spells; Bonus Feats; 3rd and 6th-level Bonus Feats; Channel Energy" e="Armorers of exquisite skill, forgepriests take inspiration from their deity to produce the most perfect weapons and armor they can, the better to equip the armies of the faithful."}

::archetype[Jistkan Magistrate]{c=warpriest r="Class Skills; Blessings; Spontaneous Casting; Bonus Languages; Channel Energy" e="Some warpriests study the ancient magic that the Jistkan magistrates used to bind elementals and make pacts with genies."}

::archetype[Liberty's Blade]{c=warpriest r="Blessings; Sacred Weapon; Channel Energy; Sacred Armor" e="There are some who find in the call to freedom something primal and spiritual, even more so than other Andorens."}

::archetype[Mantis Zealot]{c=warpriest r="Deity; Alignment; Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor; Aspect of War" e="Among the Red Mantis worshipers of Achaekek, some hold such strong faith in their assassin god that they gain divine power."}

::archetype[Molthuni Arsenal Chaplain]{c=warpriest r="Blessing; Sacred Armor; Sacred Weapon; Channel Energy" e="Molthuni arsenal chaplains are warpriests trained in the Arsenal District in Korholm. They bolster their nation's military by focusing on the militant aspects of their gods."}

::archetype[Proclaimer]{c=warpriest r="Alignment; Deity; Spellcasting; Fervor; Sacred Armor; Channel Energy" e="A proclaimer rushes into Abyss-twisted wastes with nothing but his weapon in his hand and his faith in his heart, shouting his deity's name to the skies"}

::archetype[Proselytizer]{c=warpriest r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Sacred Armor; 3rd-level Bonus Feat; Sacred Weapon; Blessings; Blessings (Major)" e="The proselytizer is the perfect militant missionary. He seeks to win the hearts and lay claim to the souls of those he meets, and - failing that - spreads the word of his deity at the edge of the sword."}

::archetype[Sacred Fist]{c=warpriest r="Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Sacred Weapon; Focus Weapon; 3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th, and 18th-level Bonus Feats; Sacred Armor" e="Unlike many warpriests, sacred fists leave behind armor and shield and instead rely on their fists and whatever protection their deity bestows on them."}

::archetype[Shieldbearer]{c=warpriest r="Focus Weapon; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor" e="Shieldbearers are divine warriors who stand on the front lines of battle, shoulder to shoulder with the soldiers of their people."}

::archetype[Sixth Wing Bulwark]{c=warpriest r="Armor Proficiencies; Focus Weapon; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor; 6-th, 12th-, 18th-level Bonus Feats" e="The Sixth Wing bulwark are followers of Ragathiel that train to hold the line and defend against the incursion of evil forces, fortifying herself and her allies to stand strong in the face of any opposition, no matter how dire."}
